summary.firstBuildTargetRead: Reference-spline plus spacing now has a target-fit signal, but visual-presence, expected-label preservation, and human-perfect scoreability still block runtime promotion. Stay on reference-spline-fit and add visual-presence-safe phase/order constraints before another promotion attempt.
summary.read: The latest sweep shows lead-in continuity and centerline spacing can produce measured readability gains, and reference-spline composition can materially improve target-video fit. Runtime promotion is still blocked because the reference-spline family fails visual-presence and trades away expected-label score and professional perfect-route potential; the next grammar pass should add phase/order, path normalization, and scoreable-window constraints around that family.

measurementLimits: This is a design-and-measurement backlog, not a runtime promotion.
Priority is derived from existing sweep blockers and target contracts; it should be recalculated after each candidate implementation.
The primitive vocabulary is intentionally game-generic so it can support Galaxy Guardians and future games as ingestion improves.

primitives.name: Visible Lead-In Continuity
primitives.playerMeaning: Aliens should enter as readable groups from offscreen or the playfield edge, not appear suddenly in the middle of the board.
successCriteria: Each group has a visible pre-contact path segment before entering the scoreable band.
No group starts with more than 20% of members already inside the central playfield without prior lead-in frames.
Human-visible arrival-continuity guardrail is >= 0.8 for any promoted candidate.
primitives.implementationPlan: Add a reusable lead-in phase before challenge group path playback; keep it data-driven so main-level entry groups can reuse it.

evidenceSummary.read: Visible Lead-In Continuity is backed by 25 group contract(s) and 25 reference path(s); blocked stages: 3, 7, 11, 15, 19.

primitives.name: Group Spacing Field
primitives.playerMeaning: A challenge group should read as a flock, not a smeared pile of sprites or overlapping pairs.
successCriteria: Human-visible spacing score is >= 0.35 and bunching risk is <= 0.55 for promoted candidates.
Group envelopes preserve enough separation to identify alien families during the pass.
Formation member offsets are stable across the path and do not collapse at high speed.
primitives.implementationPlan: Represent each group path as a centerline plus stable member offsets; clamp speed/path parameters that collapse the offsets.

evidenceSummary.read: Group Spacing Field is backed by 25 group contract(s) and 25 reference path(s); blocked stages: 3, 7, 11, 15, 19.

primitives.name: Reference Spline Fit
primitives.playerMeaning: The motion should look authored from the target examples rather than a generic sine-wave remix.
successCriteria: Target-video object fit improves without expected-label or human-visible regression.
Each group path can replay normalized reference points with bounded smoothing.
Best reference identity remains the intended challenge family.
primitives.implementationPlan: Introduce a normalized path-player primitive that samples ingested reference points and composes scale/time/offset per group.

evidenceSummary.read: Reference Spline Fit is backed by 25 group contract(s) and 25 reference path(s); blocked stages: 3, 7, 11, 15, 19.

primitives.name: Phase-Order Scheduler
primitives.playerMeaning: Challenge waves should have a coherent order and rhythm so the player can learn a perfect route.
successCriteria: Stage contact sheets show ordered group arrivals rather than simultaneous clutter.
Professional persona perfect potential does not regress after timing changes.
Group overlap windows are explicit and capped by stage contract.
primitives.implementationPlan: Make challenge timing a first-class schedule independent of path shape so future games can vary rhythm without rewriting movement logic.

evidenceSummary.read: Phase-Order Scheduler is backed by 25 group contract(s) and 25 reference path(s); blocked stages: 3, 7, 11, 15, 19.

primitives.name: Novelty Family Cascade
primitives.playerMeaning: Challenge stages should teach the player that new alien families and bonus targets matter.
successCriteria: Stage 19 presents alternating specialty families clearly enough to identify.
Challenge-specialty sprite authority improves from the current weak baseline.
Family changes are scheduled and not merely random recolors.
primitives.implementationPlan: Tie movement groups to explicit visual-family contracts so Aurora themes can vary aliens while keeping target-era grammar.

evidenceSummary.read: Novelty Family Cascade is backed by 5 group contract(s) and 5 reference path(s); blocked stages: 19.

primitives.name: Serpentine Cascade
primitives.playerMeaning: A later challenge should introduce a new spectacle, not feel like a faster early challenge.
successCriteria: Stage 15 gains a distinct serpentine multi-group path family.
Target-video object fit improves without wrong-reference identity drift.
Alien family novelty and visual readability both improve.
primitives.implementationPlan: Add a late-stage cascade primitive with group-level phase offsets and visual-family contracts.

evidenceSummary.read: Serpentine Cascade is backed by 5 group contract(s) and 5 reference path(s); blocked stages: 15.

primitives.name: Lower-Field Scoreable Pass
primitives.playerMeaning: The player should get memorable shot windows that are possible to perfect, not just watch aliens pass out of reach.
successCriteria: Reference-backed lower-field share is preserved within stage tolerance.
Human-perfect potential is >= baseline and professional watch mode can route the pass.
The pass is visible long enough for a human to aim while staying safe.
primitives.implementationPlan: Add a stage-contract field for scoreable lower-band dwell and teach persona scoring probes to value it.

evidenceSummary.read: Lower-Field Scoreable Pass is backed by 25 group contract(s) and 25 reference path(s); blocked stages: 3, 7, 11, 15, 19.

primitives.name: Hook Return Arc
primitives.playerMeaning: Hooking return arcs create the classic learnable flourish that separates later challenges from straight-line entries.
successCriteria: Path reversal count and turn count match the target-control band.
Hook returns do not bunch groups at the apex.
Exit continuity keeps the group visible through the return.
primitives.implementationPlan: Convert current side-hook variants into a parameterized arc primitive with spacing-aware apex handling.

evidenceSummary.read: Hook Return Arc is backed by 15 group contract(s) and 15 reference path(s); blocked stages: 3, 7, 11.

primitives.name: Exit Continuity
primitives.playerMeaning: Aliens should leave as part of the choreography, not vanish once the scoring opportunity ends.
successCriteria: Groups remain visible until they exit the playfield or settle into a documented transition.
No disappearance occurs before the path reaches its exit condition.
End-of-group timing remains compatible with the next wave.
primitives.implementationPlan: Replace challenge retire-on-window behavior with data-driven path completion and explicit offscreen exits.

evidenceSummary.read: Exit Continuity is backed by 25 group contract(s) and 25 reference path(s); blocked stages: 3, 7, 11, 15, 19.

primitives.name: Persona Perfect-Route Probe
primitives.playerMeaning: A professional watch run should prove the stage is learnable and scoreable before we ship it.
successCriteria: Professional persona can attempt every group in the challenge-stage tour.
Perfect-potential score does not regress when path readability improves.
Capture/rescue behavior remains irrelevant during safe challenge stages.
primitives.implementationPlan: Add per-primitive route probes so candidate sweeps score player opportunity, not only visual similarity.

evidenceSummary.read: Persona Perfect-Route Probe is backed by 25 group contract(s) and 25 reference path(s); blocked stages: 3, 7, 11, 15, 19.

stageRoadmap.label: Challenging Stage 3-4
stageRoadmap.playerMeaning: Teach that a challenging stage is safe, scoreable, and learnable rather than a combat wave.
stageRoadmap.currentBlocker: No runtime keeper: candidate regresses human-visible challenge readability by 0.2/10 or fails visibility/arrival/spacing/bunching gates.
stageRoadmap.desiredOutcome: Make Challenging Stage 3-4 visibly arrive, spread, score, and exit as a coherent Galaga-like set piece while preserving perfect-score potential.
stageRoadmap.nextAction: Prototype lead-in continuity plus group-spacing field on the first challenge before broadening runtime promotion.

stageRoadmap.label: Challenging Stage 7-8
stageRoadmap.playerMeaning: Show that the second challenge has a new route vocabulary and not just faster copies of the first.
stageRoadmap.currentBlocker: No runtime keeper: candidate regresses human-visible challenge readability by 2.3/10 or fails visibility/arrival/spacing/bunching gates.
stageRoadmap.desiredOutcome: Make Challenging Stage 7-8 visibly arrive, spread, score, and exit as a coherent Galaga-like set piece while preserving perfect-score potential.
stageRoadmap.nextAction: Wait for the Stage 3 primitive prototype, then retune this stage using the same primitive vocabulary.

stageRoadmap.label: Challenging Stage 11-12
stageRoadmap.playerMeaning: Make the player read larger boss-led and specialty shapes while still preserving a perfect-score route.
stageRoadmap.currentBlocker: No runtime keeper: candidate regresses human-visible challenge readability by 0.1/10 or fails visibility/arrival/spacing/bunching gates.
stageRoadmap.desiredOutcome: Make Challenging Stage 11-12 visibly arrive, spread, score, and exit as a coherent Galaga-like set piece while preserving perfect-score potential.
stageRoadmap.nextAction: Wait for the Stage 3 primitive prototype, then retune this stage using the same primitive vocabulary.

stageRoadmap.label: Challenging Stage 15-16
stageRoadmap.playerMeaning: Introduce a late-stage spectacle that feels authored and memorable, not a remix of early challenge stages.
stageRoadmap.currentBlocker: No runtime keeper: candidate regresses human-visible challenge readability by 0.4/10 or fails visibility/arrival/spacing/bunching gates.
stageRoadmap.desiredOutcome: Make Challenging Stage 15-16 visibly arrive, spread, score, and exit as a coherent Galaga-like set piece while preserving perfect-score potential.
stageRoadmap.nextAction: Wait for the Stage 3 primitive prototype, then retune this stage using the same primitive vocabulary.

stageRoadmap.label: Challenging Stage 19-20
stageRoadmap.playerMeaning: Prove later challenge stages have distinct progression and can be learned as separate bonus patterns.
stageRoadmap.currentBlocker: No runtime keeper: candidate regresses human-visible challenge readability by 0.3/10 or fails visibility/arrival/spacing/bunching gates.
stageRoadmap.desiredOutcome: Make Challenging Stage 19-20 visibly arrive, spread, score, and exit as a coherent Galaga-like set piece while preserving perfect-score potential.
stageRoadmap.nextAction: Wait for the Stage 3 primitive prototype, then retune this stage using the same primitive vocabulary.

leadInPrototypeEvidence.read: Latest lead-in prototype stage7-route-leadin-stair-step-lead078-flat-fan-sd018 reduced magic appearance risk to 0.072 with arrival continuity 0.928, but bunching remains 0.75 and blocks promotion.

spacingFieldPrototypeEvidence.read: Latest centerline spacing prototype stage7-centerline-spacing-spread-left-right-lead078-flat-readablefield-refwide-fan-sd014 improved human-visible readability to 7.9/10 with bunching risk 0.6, spacing 0.507, and magic risk 0.072. It is still not runtime-ready because target-video fit is 4/10 and expected-label lift is not material.

referenceSplinePrototypeEvidence.read: Latest reference-spline plus spacing prototype stage7-target-reference-paths-spacing-source-direct-widefield-refwide-p0 improved target-video object fit by 0.8/10 and readability to 8.6/10, but visual-presence, expected-label, and perfect-route gates still block promotion.

nextBestStep: Add visual-presence-safe phase-order constraints, reference-path normalization, and scoreable-window preservation around the best reference-spline plus spacing candidate, then accept only if target-video lift remains >=0.55/10, visual presence passes, expected-label regression is within -0.15/10, and human-perfect potential is preserved.
